chapter_title: Admin. Adjudication of Vehicle Parking & Stopping Offenses
text:
An ordinance, resolution, rule, or order adopted under this chapter must provide that a person charged with violating a parking or stopping ordinance, resolution, rule, or order is entitled to a hearing and provide for:

(1) the period during which a hearing must be held;

(2) the appointment of a hearing officer with authority to administer oaths and issue orders compelling the attendance of witnesses and the production of documents; and

(3) the amount and disposition of civil fines, costs, and fees.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 682. Admin. Adjudication of Vehicle Parking & Stopping Offenses
##### § 682.004 — Content of Ordinance

An ordinance, resolution, rule, or order adopted under this chapter must provide that a person charged with violating a parking or stopping ordinance, resolution, rule, or order is entitled to a hearing and provide for:

    (1) the period during which a hearing must be held;

    (2) the appointment of a hearing officer with authority to administer oaths and issue orders compelling the attendance of witnesses and the production of documents; and

    (3) the amount and disposition of civil fines, costs, and fees.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by Acts 1999, 76th Leg., ch. 156, Sec. 2, eff. May 21, 1999.*

subsections.text: An ordinance, resolution, rule, or order adopted under this chapter must provide that a person charged with violating a parking or stopping ordinance, resolution, rule, or order is entitled to a hearing and provide for:
the period during which a hearing must be held;
the appointment of a hearing officer with authority to administer oaths and issue orders compelling the attendance of witnesses and the production of documents; and
the amount and disposition of civil fines, costs, and fees.
